channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mwanza Region's Educational and Sports Achievements Highlighted
item.description: Mwanza Region in Tanzania has solidified its position as a national leader in education and sports after securing its fifth consecutive victory in the Inter-Secondary School tournament. Regional Commi...

item.title: Patricia Greene, longtime voice of Jill Archer in BBC Radio 4's The Archers, dies aged 94
item.description: Patricia Greene, affectionately known as Paddy Greene, has died at the age of 94 after a remarkable career spanning almost seven decades as Jill Archer in BBC Radio 4's The Archers. Joining the progra...

item.title: Scientists Solve Dendrite-Induced Short Circuits in Solid-State Batteries
item.description: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials have identified how soft lithium dendrites penetrate hard ceramic electrolytes in solid-state batteries, causing short circuits. This ...

item.title: Nova Scotia seeks proposals to determine future of Halifax's Mackay Bridge
item.description: Nova Scotia has issued a request for proposals (RFP) to explore the potential future of Halifax’s Mackay Bridge, a key infrastructure project in the province. The initiative aims to assess options for...

item.title: Caleb Amisi Warns Irungu Kang'ata Against Taking Over Linda Mwananchi Movement
item.description: Saboti MP Caleb Amisi has publicly criticized Murang'a Governor Irungu Kang'ata for attempting to take control of the Linda Mwananchi political movement. Amisi accused Kang'ata of having ulterior moti...

item.title: A Man of the People: A Critical Analysis of Corruption and Leadership in Modern Africa
item.description: The article reviews Chinua Achebe’s novel 'A Man of the People,' highlighting its enduring relevance to contemporary African political issues. It examines how corruption, state capture, and unethical ...

item.title: Philippine Military Expands Security Measures to Protect Bajo de Masinloc Amid Tensions
item.description: Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. announced that the military is enhancing security measures to protect Panatag (Scarborough) Shoal and expand patrols across the Philippines' maritime territory. ...

item.title: Banjara Community Launches Awareness Campaign Against Dowry and Gender-Based Violence
item.description: The Banjara community, long plagued by the dowry system, is organizing a significant awareness program in Yadgir to combat this practice. Leaders of the All India Banjara Seva Sangha, Devraj Naik and ...

item.title: Big Four accounting firms face trust crisis as reputations crumble
item.description: The article discusses the growing trust crisis among the Big Four accounting firms—KPMG, EY, Deloitte, and PwC—as their carefully cultivated reputations face public scrutiny. KPMG Australia's leadersh...

item.title: Canadians support Carney’s economic management despite weak growth
item.description: A Nanos Research Group poll for Bloomberg News reveals that 60% of Canadians believe Mark Carney, Canada's prime minister, has performed well in managing the economy, even though his first year in off...

item.title: Uganda repatriates 1,102 nationals amid xenophobic violence in South Africa
item.description: Uganda has completed the first phase of repatriating 1,102 citizens from South Africa following xenophobic violence and anti-migrant protests. The final evacuation flight, carrying 219 evacuees, lande...

item.title: Heat Stress and Climate Challenges Threaten World Cup Match in Miami
item.description: The upcoming World Cup quarter-final between England and Norway in Miami faces extreme heat stress due to a combination of high temperatures, humidity, and Saharan dust. Scientists warn that the Wet B...

item.title: Tanzania Universities Commission Launches First-Degree Admissions Process
item.description: The Tanzania Commission for Universities (TCU) has initiated the application process for first-degree admissions, offering thousands of students access to higher education. This marks a critical perio...

item.title: Raila Educational Centre in Kibra Receives New Classrooms and Teacher Employment Pledges
item.description: President William Ruto visited Raila Educational Centre in Kibra, Nairobi, to hand over eight new classrooms and pledge government employment for six teachers. The school, established in 2016 with sup...

item.title: Marcos Jr. Reiterates Commitment to Peaceful Resolution of South China Sea Disputes
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. reiterated on the anniversary of the 2016 arbitral award that the South China Sea must remain a zone of cooperation rather than conflict. During a press conference, he e...

item.title: High Court rules against deportation of migrants in legal challenge to Home Secretary's policy
item.description: The High Court has ruled in favour of five Channel migrants who challenged the legality of their deportation under the one-in, one-out deal with France. The migrants, who claimed to be victims of traf...

item.title: Chinese activist detained in Thailand faces deportation to China after last-minute visa issues
item.description: Zhang Xinyan, a Chinese activist and Falun Gong practitioner, was detained in Thailand after being blocked from flying to Vancouver. She had been granted refugee status by the United Nations and was i...

item.title: Birds That Intentionally Spread Wildfires: The Firehawk Phenomenon
item.description: The article explores the behavior of three Australian bird species—black kite, whistling kite, and brown falcon—that intentionally spread wildfires by carrying burning sticks across firebreaks. Aborig...

item.title: Internal Party Conflicts and Leadership Challenges in Trinamool Congress
item.description: The Trinamool Congress (TMC), once the dominant political force in West Bengal, is facing a severe crisis marked by mass defections and internal rebellion. A significant number of MPs, including promi...

item.title: Wall Street's Shift to Political Favor Risks Economic Stability
item.description: The article highlights how the U.S. stock market has become increasingly influenced by President Donald Trump's political decisions rather than traditional economic fundamentals. Finance professionals...
